content.text:
Looking at the image, the track curves to the left ahead with the car positioned toward the right side of the track (gravel visible on the right), and the car needs to continue turning left to follow the curve.

**Waypoint Analysis:**
- nearest_waypoints[1] = 100, target = (100 + 5) % 120 = 105
- Waypoint 105: x=1.0943, y=1.6655
- Car: x=0.8319, y=1.7781
- Bearing = atan2(1.6655 - 1.7781, 1.0943 - 0.8319) = atan2(-0.1126, 0.2624) ≈ -23.2°
- Current heading: -85.18°
- Delta = -23.2° - (-85.18°) = +61.98° → steer LEFT (sharp)

```json
{
  "reasoning": "Track curves sharply left ahead with car right of center near gravel; delta of +62° to target waypoint 105 requires sharp left steering to follow the loop back northeast.",
  "knowledge": [
    "Track loops sharply south then back northeast around waypoints 95-110",
    "Positive delta means steer left (positive steering_angle)",
    "At delta > 50°, use speed=1.0 and steering=+20° to stay on track",
    "Car is right of center near gravel — follow waypoints to pull back left",
    "Track width 0.76m — narrow, maintain control through turns",
    "After southward section, waypoints loop back east then north",
    "Avoid overcorrecting — once delta < 5°, reduce steering to 0°",
    "Car heading around -85° (mostly south), needs to swing toward northeast",
    "Progress at 84.7% — approaching final section of lap, stay smooth",
    "Sharp left correction needed for several turns to follow the loop back"
  ],
  "movement_analysis": {
    "x": [0.779, 0.788, 0.806, 0.832],
    "y": [1.965, 1.906, 1.839, 1.778],
    "yaw": [-99.2, -95.4, -90.7, -85.2]
  },
  "waypoint_analysis": 61.98,
  "speed": 1.0,
  "steering_angle": 20.0
}
```
